title: SpaceX Investors at US Retail Brokers Got at Least One IPO Share
summary: Customers at some of the largest retail brokerage firms in the US each received at least a single share in SpaceX’s $86.2 billion IPO, underscoring how the offering was designed to give individual investors a sizable role.

title: SpaceX Set to Overtake Amazon in Value as It Soars for Third Day
summary: SpaceX shares surged in premarket trading, putting the firm on track to overtake Amazon.com Inc. as the fifth largest publicly traded company in the world just days after its blockbuster debut.

title: Stocks Drift After Three-Day Rally; SpaceX Surges: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks paused after a three-day rally with investors shifting their focus to a raft of central bank decisions this week. SpaceX continued to surge, on track for a more than 50% jump since going public.

title: Nike Can’t Afford to Lose This World Cup
summary: Nike has the star power and home advantage. But with its turnaround moving slowly, the World Cup may matter more to Nike than Adidas, explains Bloomberg Opinion columnist Andrea Felsted. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hormuz Reopening Seen Unleashing Pent-Up Gulf Grain Demand
summary: An interim US-Iran deal to end their months-long war and potentially fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz is likely to trigger a rush of grain imports to nations in the Persian Gulf after months of reliance on longer, less efficient routes.

title: Bondholders Forced to Reckon With $4 Billion Tax Threat in Bitter Credit Feud
summary: Patrick Drahi was running out of options to save his debt-ridden telecom empire.

title: 1MDB Theft Made Attila the Hun Look Like ‘Choirboy,’ Judge Says
summary: The judge who convicted former Malaysian Prime Minister Najib Razak over the 1MDB scandal said the plunder of the sovereign wealth fund was so vast that it eclipsed the exploits of Attila the Hun, one of history’s most notorious conquerors.

title: Dolce & Gabbana Weighs Sale of Milan Properties to Raise Cash
summary: Dolce & Gabbana is negotiating with lenders over the potential sale of real estate assets in Milan as part of an effort to raise liquidity and refinance debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Middle East Oil Collapses as Contango Returns on Supply Hope
summary: Middle Eastern crude markets have collapsed, as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz spurs optimism around an increase in flows from the oil-rich region.

title: Verizon Debuts New Plan, Loyalty Program in Battle for Customers
summary: The freebies competition is heating up among US wireless carriers.

title: Qatar Plans to Rapidly Restart LNG Output After Hormuz Opens
summary: Qatar is planning to rapidly boost liquefied natural gas production once the Strait of Hormuz reopens, aiming to restore most of its export capacity within two months, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Biocon's Mazumdar-Shaw Says Firm On Track To Be Top Insulin Maker
summary: Biocon Executive Chairperson Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw says she is confident that the company's investment and capacity expansion in Insulin will make the company the top hub for the drug in the next five years. She speaks in an exclusive conversation with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Empty Tankers Entering Gulf Via Hormuz Are Key for Energy Market
summary: The transit of empty ships through the Strait of Hormuz into the energy-rich Persian Gulf is being eagerly watched by the market, which would indicate increasing confidence in the outlook for a sustained peace deal.

title: EBA Eyes Bigger Role for Banks in Setting Key Capital Buffer
summary: Europe’s top banking regulator said watchdogs should rely more on banks to calculate how big of a safety cushion they need on top of their minimum capital requirements.

title: SpaceX Shares Surge, US & Iran Prepare for Deal Signing | The Opening Trade 6/16/2026
summary:
The US and Iran are preparing to formally sign their interim peace deal in Switzerland on Friday, with both sides claiming victory and oil traders and shippers unsure over how quickly the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened.

SpaceX shares surged in premarket trading, putting the stock on track to extend a rally following its blockbuster debut last week.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Prospect of a Quieter Fed Could Roil Bond Prices
summary: Warsh has hinted at less communication from THE central bank.

title: SBI Plans Indian Rupee Deposits in Sri Lanka to Support Trade
summary: State Bank of India is planning to introduce Indian rupee-denominated deposits in Sri Lanka, a move aimed at encouraging wider use of the currency in trade and investment between the neighboring nations.

title: Glencore, Trafigura Said Set to Invest in Merdeka’s HK Listing
summary: Glencore Plc, Mercuria Energy Group and Trafigura Group are among key investors planning to buy shares in the Hong Kong listing of Indonesian gold miner PT Merdeka Gold Resources, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Don’t Get Overexcited on Oil Price Moderation, AIB’s Hunt Says
summary: The global economy still faces multiple risks even as the US and Iran agree to halt the war in the Middle East, the head of Irish bank AIB Group Plc warned.

title: Turkey Seeks Higher Oil Flows Via Iraq Pipeline to Renew Deal
summary: Turkey is seeking higher oil flows through a crucial pipeline that runs through its territory from northern Iraq in order to renew a contract that expires in a little over a month, according to a Turkish official with direct knowledge of the matter.

title: Riksbank Rate Seen Steady With Norway on Edge: Decision Guide
summary: Sweden and Norway face contrasting economic risks, but the two Nordic neighbors are still likely to emerge with the same outcome of no change for borrowing costs this week.

title: Warsh Wants Less Fed Talk, Risking More Market Surprises
summary: Former Federal Reserve Chair Ben Bernanke famously said monetary policy is 98% talk and only 2% action. Kevin Warsh wants to shake up the mix.

title: Blackstone Grows Asset-Based Lending With New Unit, Taps Garlick
summary: Blackstone Inc. rolled out a platform to lend against physical assets like inventory and equipment, as alternative asset managers increasingly muscle into territory long dominated by banks.

title: Reeves Says Her Plans Will Make UK Less Reliant on Bond Markets
summary: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said her ambition is to balance the budget in 2029, a more demanding target than her current fiscal rules as she pledged to make Britain less at the mercy of the bond markets.

title: Thames Water Edges Closer to Nationalization With Rescue in Doubt
summary: Thames Water moved closer to temporary nationalization after a senior government minister said a rescue deal put forward by creditors wasn’t sufficient.

title: German Investor Outlook Jumps With Iran Resolution in Sight
summary: German investor optimism improved more than anticipated on hopes that a swift end to the Iran war will ease pressure on Europe’s largest economy.

title: Jeff Currie Says Hormuz Flows May Not Normalize Until End of the Year
summary: Jeff Currie, executive co-chairman at Abaxx Markets, says “the uncertainty remains quite high” around the Strait of Hormuz as maintaining the US-Iran ceasefire will be challenging. Currie looks at what the interim agreement means for the global oil market, what’s behind the oil price drop, and how quickly Gulf nations can restore production. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Taiwan Frees Life Insurers to Deploy Capital in AI Projects
summary: Taiwan is easing regulations to give life insurers flexibility to invest in artificial intelligence projects, part of an ongoing campaign by authorities to redirect a larger slice of the industry’s $1 trillion asset pool back home.

title: Emerging Stocks Near Record High on Tech Gains, Lower Oil Prices
summary: Emerging-market stocks climbed for a third day, holding near a record high, as crude prices fell further and the tech rally extended.

title: AI Stock Rally Is in Boom Phase Not Euphoria, BofA Survey Shows
summary: The boom in artificial-intelligence stocks looks set to run further, according to a Bank of America Corp. survey, which found the majority of investors believe that the fear of missing out is continuing to drive the trade.

title: Bonds Extend Rally as Oil Falls to Lowest Since March
